tldr: Cerbos handles authorization policies while Corbado focuses on passkey-based authentication, so this is a category shift rather than a feature-for-feature swap.
why_switch: You are prioritizing passwordless/passkey login and dropping a custom authz layer
You want a managed authentication SaaS rather than a self-hosted PDP
Your authorization needs have simplified to a few static roles
You want to reduce infrastructure surface area
Compliance or UX pressure pushes you toward passkeys
what_to_export: List of integration points calling the PDP
Any custom data sources used by Cerbos
export_method: Cerbos policies are file-based YAML stored in your repository — archive that directory plus any test fixtures. For decision logs, follow the Cerbos official docs for the audit log exporter.
import_method: Corbado is an authentication provider, so Cerbos data does not map onto it. Set up Corbado for sign-in/passkey flows per Corbado official docs and reimplement authorization checks in your application or in a separate authz tool.
gotchas: Removing Cerbos without a replacement leaves authorization undefined
Passkey adoption needs UX planning and fallback flows
Corbado pricing is not fully transparent — get a quote before commitment
Plan account recovery and device-lost flows for passkeys
Verify regional availability and compliance for your user base

email.subject: Cancellation: Cerbos Cloud / commercial plan
email.body:
Hi Cerbos team,

We are simplifying our identity stack and moving authentication to Corbado. We would like to wind down our Cerbos commercial usage — please confirm the cancellation procedure, any final invoices, and any export we should run before account closure.

Thanks for the support.

Best regards
